_note: SLAB c.1600-1500 BC — the Near Eastern great-power system after the Hittite sack of Babylon: MITANNI's rise, KASSITE Babylon, the Hittite Middle-Kingdom 'dark age', and ELAM. Four succession king-lists, each with '—' placeholder spouses; REAL father-son links are distinguished from succession/usurpation in every label, and every uncertain link is flagged. Web-verified against Wikipedia ('Mitanni', 'Shaushtatar', 'Kassite dynasty', 'Burnaburiash I', 'Hantili II', 'Alluwamna', 'List of Hittite kings', 'List of Elamite kings', 'Kutir-Nahhunte I') plus Britannica ('Karaindash', 'Kutir-Nahhunte I') and World History Encyclopedia. THREAD 1 MITANNI (MIT_, a NEW Hurrian house of the upper Euphrates): Kirta (SEMI-LEGENDARY founder, defined here as a ROOT node for the main agent to attach as a fresh house) > Shuttarna I (REAL son of Kirta per the sources) > Parrattarna / Parshatatar / Barattarna (relation to Shuttarna I not documented — early Mitannian succession is uncertain, flagged; he expanded Mitanni to dominate Syria, took Aleppo & made Idrimi of Alalakh a vassal, and pressed on Assur; Barattarna and Parshatatar may be one king) > Shaushtatar (REAL son of Parshatatar; conquered Alalakh, Nuzi, Kizzuwatna & sacked Assur, the empire's high-water mark). THREAD 2 KASSITE BABYLON (BAB_, extends the existing BAB_KASAGUM2 = Agum II 'Agum-Kakrime', referenced as a string, NOT recreated): NOTE the task's suggested year for Burnaburiash I (c.1530) is LARGER than the existing Agum II node's c.1500, which would break the child-year-smaller rule, so the successors are dated descending from c.1500 (Kassite absolute chronology is genuinely fuzzy; king-list ORDER is what is anchored). Burnaburiash I (first Kassite to really rule Babylon proper; descent from Agum II not established — succession, flagged) > Kashtiliash III (REAL son of Burnaburiash I) > Ulamburiash (REAL son of Burnaburiash I & thus brother of Kashtiliash III, shown as his successor; conquered the First Sealand Dynasty) > Agum III (REAL son of Kashtiliash III & grandson of Burnaburiash I, shown as successor to his uncle Ulamburiash; campaigned again against the Sealand) > Kadashman-harbe I (relation to Agum III not documented — succession, flagged) > Karaindash (c.1415; relation uncertain, flagged; made the border treaty with Assyria's Ashur-bel-nisheshu). THREAD 3 HITTITE MIDDLE KINGDOM (HIT_, extends the existing HIT_TELIPINU, referenced as a string, NOT recreated): a poorly-documented, usurpation-ridden stretch whose very ORDER is disputed. Alluwamna (NOT descent: son-in-law of Telipinu, married his daughter Harapseki — succession by marriage) > Tahurwaili (a usurper; his placement in the sequence is uncertain — most reconstructions put him before or around Alluwamna, kept here in the task's order but flagged) > Hantili II (REAL son of Alluwamna, attested in a land grant) > Zidanta II (succeeded Hantili II, relation undocumented — succession, flagged; assassinated) > Huzziya II (LIKELY son of Zidanta II — probable descent, flagged) > Muwatalli I (NOT descent: assassinated Huzziya II to seize the throne — usurpation). THREAD 4 ELAM (EL_, extends the existing EL_KUDUZULUSH1 = Kuduzulush I, referenced as a string, NOT recreated): Kutir-Nahhunte I (late-Sukkalmah king, c.1730-1715, famed for a raid deep into Babylonia; the intervening Sukkalmah rulers between Kuduzulush I and him are omitted, and the avuncular Sukkalmah succession means descent is not established — succession, flagged) > then a large dynastic gap of ~230 yrs to the KIDINUID dynasty (c.1500-1400), whose five kings are grouped for convenience only — there is NO evidence they were related to one another: Kidinu (traditionally earliest — succession-by-convention, NOT descent, flagged) > Inshushinak-sunkir-nappipir (Kidinuid; no attested kin link — NOT descent, flagged) > Tan-Ruhuratir II (Kidinuid; no attested kin link — NOT descent, flagged). All dates lead each label and DECREASE down every chain; no fabrication — only names, relationships and dates found in the cited sources, with every uncertainty flagged in-node.

a.label:
👑 Kirta
c.1500 BC · semi-legendary founder of Mitanni, the Hurrian kingdom of the upper Euphrates (ROOT — main agent to attach as a fresh house) · SEMI-LEGENDARY, dates & historicity uncertain

children.label:
👑 Shuttarna I
c.1490 BC · king of Mitanni; son of Kirta (REAL father-son per the king-list tradition); left a royal seal reused by later Mitannian kings · historical (early Mitannian succession uncertain)

👑 Parrattarna (Parshatatar / Barattarna)
c.1480 BC · king of Mitanni; relation to Shuttarna I not documented — early Mitannian succession uncertain; expanded Mitanni to dominate Syria, took Aleppo & made Idrimi of Alalakh his vassal, pressing on Assur (Barattarna & Parshatatar may be one king) · historical

👑 Shaushtatar
c.1440 BC · king of Mitanni; son of Parshatatar (REAL father-son); conquered Alalakh, Nuzi & Kizzuwatna and sacked Assur, bringing Mitanni to its greatest extent · historical

👑 Burnaburiash I
c.1490 BC · Kassite king, the first to really rule Babylon proper; descent from Agum II not established — succession (dates set descending from the existing Agum II node; Kassite absolute chronology is fuzzy) · historical

👑 Kashtiliash III
c.1470 BC · Kassite king of Babylon; son of Burnaburiash I (REAL father-son) · historical

👑 Ulamburiash
c.1460 BC · Kassite king; son of Burnaburiash I & brother of Kashtiliash III (REAL descent, shown as his brother's successor); conquered the First Sealand Dynasty of southern Babylonia · historical

👑 Agum III
c.1445 BC · Kassite king; son of Kashtiliash III & grandson of Burnaburiash I (REAL descent, shown as successor to his uncle Ulamburiash); campaigned again against the Sealand to re-unite the land · historical

👑 Kadashman-harbe I
c.1430 BC · Kassite king of Babylon; relation to Agum III not documented — succession · historical

👑 Karaindash
c.1415 BC · Kassite king of Babylon; relation to predecessor uncertain; made the earliest attested Babylonian-Assyrian border treaty (with Ashur-bel-nisheshu of Assyria) · historical

👑 Alluwamna
c.1500 BC · Hittite king; not a descendant but son-in-law of Telipinu (married his daughter Harapseki) — succession by marriage · historical (Middle-Kingdom succession obscure)

👑 Tahurwaili
c.1490 BC · Hittite king & usurper; his place in the sequence is disputed (many reconstructions put him before or around Alluwamna) — position & kinship uncertain · historical

👑 Hantili II
c.1480 BC · Hittite king; son of Alluwamna (REAL father-son, attested in a land-grant document), shown here after the usurper Tahurwaili · historical

👑 Zidanta II
c.1475 BC · Hittite king; succeeded Hantili II, relation not documented — succession; his brief reign ended in assassination · historical

👑 Huzziya II
c.1470 BC · Hittite king; likely son of Zidanta II (probable father-son, flagged) · historical

👑 Muwatalli I
c.1465 BC · Hittite king; not a descendant — assassinated Huzziya II to seize the throne (usurpation); himself later killed, closing the turbulent Middle Kingdom · historical

👑 Kutir-Nahhunte I
c.1730 BC · late-Sukkalmah king of Elam (r. c.1730-1715); famed for a deep raid into Babylonia (recalled by Ashurbanipal a millennium later); intervening Sukkalmah rulers omitted & avuncular succession means descent from Kuduzulush I is not established — succession · historical

👑 Kidinu
c.1500 BC · founder-eponym of the Kidinuid dynasty of Elam (Middle Elamite period); follows a ~230-yr gap after the Sukkalmah era — succession-by-convention, NOT descent; titled 'king of Susa & Anshan' · historical

👑 Inshushinak-sunkir-nappipir
c.1490 BC · Kidinuid king of Elam; no attested kin link to Kidinu — the Kidinuids are grouped for convenience only, NOT a proven family · historical

👑 Tan-Ruhuratir II
c.1480 BC · Kidinuid king of Elam; no attested kin link to his predecessor — NOT descent, succession-by-convention · historical
